title: Tool Hallucination
concept: The model inventing non-existent tools or parameters when it forgets the available schema.

exercises.prompt: What happens when an AI agent gets distracted during a task?

options.text: It may drift away from the primary goal.
It becomes more efficient at completing tasks.

exercises.explain: When an AI agent gets distracted, it can begin to focus on unimportant details, leading it away from its main goal.

cards.title: What is Tool Hallucination?
cards.body: Tool hallucination occurs when an AI agent invents tools or actions that don't actually exist because it forgets what tools are available.

cards.title: Definition
cards.body: Tool hallucination is when artificial intelligence creates imaginary tools or parameters that do not exist, often due to misunderstanding or forgetting the correct options.

cards.title: Analogy
cards.body: Imagine a painter who forgets they have certain brushes. Instead, they invent a new brush by combining existing ones in their imagination, leading to unexpected results.

cards.title: Example
cards.body: If an AI agent is supposed to use a 'calculator' tool and instead says it can use a 'magic wand' to perform arithmetic, that's tool hallucination.

cards.title: Myth: AI Never Makes Mistakes
cards.body: Some believe AI is perfect, but it can definitely make errors, like inventing tools that aren't real when it gets confused.

cards.title: Visual Comparison
cards.body: Comparing a valid tool to a hallucinated one:

exercises.prompt: What is an example of tool hallucination in AI?

options.text: Using a calculator to perform math.
Saying it can generate a new tool that doesn't exist.
Following instructions accurately with available tools.

exercises.explain: An example of tool hallucination is when an AI claims it can use a tool that isn't available, like a 'magic wand' for math instead of a calculator.

exercises.prompt: AI agents never invent non-existent tools.
exercises.explain: False. AI agents can invent tools when they forget the available options, which is part of tool hallucination.

exercises.prompt: Tool hallucination occurs when an AI creates __________ tools that don't actually exist.
exercises.sentence: Tool hallucination occurs when an AI creates imaginary tools that don't actually exist.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'imaginary', as it describes tools that an AI mistakenly invents.

exercises.prompt: Which of these statements describes tool hallucination?

options.text: An AI uses all available tools correctly.
An AI claims it can use tools that don’t exist.
An AI is always focused on its main goal.

exercises.explain: The correct statement describes tool hallucination, where an AI mistakenly invents tools instead of using the real ones.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario best exemplifies tool hallucination in AI?

options.text: An AI agent successfully solves problems using a known algorithm.
An AI claims it can use a 'laser pointer' to solve a math problem when it actually needs a calculator.
An AI follows instructions from a provided user guide with no errors.
An AI agent develops new software without any specifications.

exercises.explain: The second scenario shows tool hallucination, where the AI incorrectly believes it has a tool ('laser pointer') that isn't suitable for the task.
